description: Test cases for iOS form filling and control interaction: ui_input (replace/append, Unicode/emoji, secure, UITextView multiline), ui_control_sendAction (UISwitch/UISlider/UIStepper/UISegmentedControl), keyboard dismissal, sync vs async submit strategies, scrollToElement for offscreen fields, and static structure checks (formerly ios-form-filling)

evals.prompt: Fill out a login form on an iOS app with username 'john.doe@example.com' and password 'SecurePass123', then tap the login button. The login is an async server call.
evals.expected_output:
Should successfully:
1. Call ui_inspect to locate username/password fields and login button (prefer accessibilityIdentifier over path)
2. Call ui_input for username with mode='replace', submit=false (intermediate field, avoid keyboard flicker)
3. Call ui_input for password with mode='replace', submit=true (last field, auto-dismiss keyboard)
4. Verify password response returns length + masked (secure field does not echo plaintext)
5. Call ui_tap on the login button (do NOT use ui_tap_and_inspect with fixed stableTimeMs — async submit would land on loading intermediate state)
6. Delegate success/fail judgment to ios-ui-wait ui_waitAny with two conditions: success = targetExists on post-login element (e.g. 'home_welcome_label'); fail = targetExists alert or textContains '错误'
7. Each ui_input ~100ms; control actions ~3ms; total fill < 1 second, wait time bounded by ui_waitAny timeout

evals.prompt: In an iOS settings screen, toggle a UISwitch with accessibility identifier 'notifications.switch', adjust a UISlider with accessibility identifier 'volume.slider' to 75%, increment a UIStepper with accessibility identifier 'count.stepper' once, and select the second segment (index 1) of a UISegmentedControl with accessibility identifier 'theme.segmented'.
evals.expected_output:
Should successfully:
1. Call ui_inspect once to capture viewSnapshotID and confirm all four controls are enabled (isEnabled==true)
2. Call ui_control_sendAction four times back-to-back with event='valueChanged' (NOT 'action'):
   - UISwitch: accessibilityIdentifier='notifications.switch', no value field (flips on/off)
   - UISlider: accessibilityIdentifier='volume.slider', value=0.75 (must be 0.0-1.0)
   - UIStepper: accessibilityIdentifier='count.stepper', no value (increments one step)
   - UISegmentedControl: accessibilityIdentifier='theme.segmented', value=1 (segment index, 0-based)
3. No need to re-inspect between the four calls (same screen, viewSnapshotID stays valid)
4. Each response returns previousValue/currentValue — verify switch flipped, slider=0.75, segment=1
5. Total ~12-16ms for all four control actions (3-4ms each)

evals.prompt: Fill a UITextView in an iOS app (verified via ui_inspect type=='UITextView') with three lines of text containing Unicode and emoji: '第一行 🎉\n第二行 ✨\n第三行', then dismiss the keyboard.
evals.expected_output:
Should successfully:
1. Call ui_inspect and confirm target type is UITextView (NOT UITextField — UITextField rejects \n with input_rejected)
2. Call ui_input with accessibilityIdentifier/path of the UITextView, text='第一行 🎉\n第二行 ✨\第三行', mode='replace', submit=false (we want manual dismiss afterward)
3. Verify response finalText preserves \n and the Unicode/emoji characters (ui_input goes through UITextInput.insertText, full UTF-8 support — not keyboard scancodes)
4. Call ui_keyboard_dismiss with strategy='auto' (default) to close the keyboard
5. Verify keyboard_dismiss response shows firstResponderAfter==null
6. Total time ~300ms (input ~93ms + dismiss ~206ms)

evals.prompt: A long iOS registration form has a 'Confirm Password' field at the bottom that is off-screen. Scroll it into view, then fill it with 'Pass123' in replace mode.
evals.expected_output:
Should successfully:
1. Call ui_scrollToElement with match='text' (or 'accessibilityIdentifier') and value='Confirm Password' (or the field's a11y id) to scroll the off-screen field into the visible region
2. Call ui_inspect to get a FRESH viewSnapshotID (the old one is now stale — scroll invalidates snapshot immediately)
3. Locate the Confirm Password field in the fresh targets array via accessibilityIdentifier or path
4. Call ui_input with mode='replace' and the fresh viewSnapshotID
5. Verify response finalText='Pass123' (or length+masked if it is a secure field)
6. Do NOT reuse the pre-scroll viewSnapshotID — that returns stale_locator

evals.prompt: Check the static structure of the ios-ui-form SKILL.md (offline, no app run): frontmatter must have name / description / allowed-tools fields present; every tool listed in allowed-tools must be a real iOSDriver MCP tool; the body must not hardcode SPMExample-specific values (com.coo.SPMExample bundle id, or specific UDIDs like 065CC8DB / 00008030); the body must not claim a specific iOS deployment target tied to SPMExample.
evals.expected_output:
Should verify offline (no app run required):
1. Frontmatter has all three fields present — grep -E '^(name|description|allowed-tools):' returns 3
2. allowed-tools lists exactly these 7 real iOSDriver tools: mcp__iOSDriver__ui_input, mcp__iOSDriver__ui_tap, mcp__iOSDriver__ui_tap_and_inspect, mcp__iOSDriver__ui_control_sendAction, mcp__iOSDriver__ui_keyboard_dismiss, mcp__iOSDriver__ui_inspect, mcp__iOSDriver__ui_scrollToElement
3. No SPMExample hardcoding in body — grep -nE 'com\.coo\.SPMExample|065CC8DB|00008030|SPMExample deployment' returns empty (OK)
4. name field value matches directory name (ios-ui-form)
5. description contains transition keyword '原 ios-form-filling' for old-prompt routing
6. Body covers preserved capabilities: text input (replace/append + Unicode/emoji), ui_control_sendAction for UISwitch/UISlider/UIStepper/UISegmentedControl, keyboard management, form submission (sync vs async), scrollToElement for offscreen fields
7. Platform constraint uses generic wording like 'iOSExploreServer 要求 iOS 15+;部署目标视宿主 App 而定' — NOT 'iOS 26.2+ required (matches the SPMExample deployment target)'
